mnemonics: PEOPLE RARELY SEARCH PERFECTLY: Use this to remember the key areas of user interaction - Presentation, Effort, Queries, Results, Searchers, Personalization, Evaluation, Long-term, Sessions, Patterns, Expectations, Relevance, Feedback, Errors, Loops, Trust, and Interfaces.
keyPoints: User interaction involves how searchers formulate queries and interact with results.
Effective interfaces provide useful feedback and support query reformulation.
Understanding user behavior and cognitive models is crucial for improving search engine design.
commonMistakes: Designing only for expert users and ignoring the needs of novice searchers.
Overlooking the importance of system feedback and transparency in building user trust.
Focusing solely on algorithmic relevance while neglecting the user's context and real information need.
